description: Add Storybook stories and E2E tests to prevent regressions in tool grouping for both BrowserToolsCard and PluginCard. The browser tool grouping was lost during the permission redesign merge because no tests verified the grouped rendering. This PRD adds visual and behavioral tests to ensure tool groups (section headers and grouped tool rows) are always rendered correctly in both cards.

userStories.title: Add Storybook stories for BrowserToolsCard grouped rendering
userStories.description: As a developer, I want Storybook stories that verify BrowserToolsCard renders tool groups correctly so that group rendering regressions are caught during development
acceptanceCriteria: BrowserToolsCard.stories.tsx has a 'WithGroups' story that renders tools with different group values (e.g., 'Tab Management', 'Page Interaction', 'Network & Storage')
The story verifies group header dividers appear with uppercase text and bg-muted/20 styling
The story verifies tools within each group are rendered after their group header
There is an 'UngroupedTools' story that renders tools without any group field — these should render as a flat list with no group headers
There is a 'MixedGroupAndUngrouped' story where some tools have groups and some don't — ungrouped tools appear under an 'Other' section at the bottom
npm run build passes
npm run type-check passes
npm run lint passes
npm run test passes
userStories.notes:
Files to modify: - platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/BrowserToolsCard.stories.tsx The BrowserToolState type (from bridge.ts) has an optional 'group?: string' field. The grouping logic in BrowserToolsCard.tsx groups tools by this field, with ungrouped tools falling into an 'Other' bucket. Group headers are rendered as plain dividers: `
GROUP NAME
`. Create mock tool data with realistic groups matching the actual browser tool groups: 'Tab Management', 'Page Interaction', 'DOM & Content', 'Forms & Input', 'Network & Storage', 'Diagnostics'.

userStories.title: Add Storybook stories for PluginCard grouped rendering
userStories.description: As a developer, I want Storybook stories that verify PluginCard renders tool groups correctly so that group rendering regressions are caught during development
acceptanceCriteria: PluginCard.stories.tsx has a 'WithGroups' story that renders a plugin with tools assigned to different groups
The story verifies group header dividers appear between tool sections — group headers are plain text labels with no interactive controls (no Switch, no selector)
There is a 'NoGroups' story that renders a plugin where no tools have a group field — tools render as a flat list
There is a 'SingleGroup' story where all tools share the same group — the single group header appears above the tools
npm run build passes
npm run type-check passes
npm run lint passes
npm run test passes
userStories.notes:
Files to modify: - platform/browser-extension/src/side-panel/components/PluginCard.stories.tsx The PluginCard group rendering logic is similar to BrowserToolsCard — it buckets tools by their 'group' field and renders a plain text divider header for each group. The group header currently has no interactive controls (Switch was removed in the permission redesign). The header is just: `
GROUP NAME
`. Use mock plugin data with groups matching real plugin patterns (e.g., Slack tools grouped into 'Messaging', 'Channels', 'Users', 'Files').

userStories.title: Add E2E test verifying browser tool groups appear in the side panel
userStories.description: As a developer, I want an E2E test that verifies browser tool groups are rendered in the live side panel so that merges that accidentally remove grouping are caught by CI
acceptanceCriteria: E2E test opens the side panel and expands the Browser tools accordion
Test verifies that at least 3 distinct group headers are visible (e.g., 'TAB MANAGEMENT', 'PAGE INTERACTION', 'NETWORK & STORAGE')
Test verifies that group headers are uppercase text elements (font-head, text-xs, uppercase styling)
Test verifies that tool rows appear below their respective group headers
Test verifies no group headers appear when all tools are filtered to a single group by the search bar
npm run build passes
npm run type-check passes
npm run lint passes
npm run test passes
npm run test:e2e passes
userStories.notes:
Files to modify:
- e2e/side-panel-browser-tool-groups.e2e.ts (new file)

The E2E test should use the standard fixture setup from e2e/fixtures.ts. The browser tools card is always visible (rendered from BROWSER_TOOLS_CATALOG at build time). The accordion item has value='browser-tools'.

Group headers can be located by their characteristic styling: elements inside the browser tools accordion that have the 'uppercase' and 'tracking-wider' CSS classes, or use text content matching known group names.

The test should also verify the regression specifically: expand the accordion, count the number of group header elements, and assert it's >= 3 (the actual count depends on how many groups exist in the catalog, but should be at least 3). This is the assertion that would have caught the permission redesign regression.

For the search/filter test: type a search term that matches tools in only one group, verify only that group's header appears.

userStories.title: Add E2E test verifying plugin tool groups appear in the side panel
userStories.description: As a developer, I want an E2E test that verifies plugin tool groups are rendered for plugins with grouped tools so that merges that accidentally remove grouping are caught by CI
acceptanceCriteria: E2E test uses the e2e-test plugin (which should have tools with group assignments) or sets up a test plugin with grouped tools
Test opens the side panel and expands the test plugin's accordion
Test verifies that group headers appear as plain text dividers (no Switch, no interactive control)
Test verifies that tool rows appear within their group sections
npm run build passes
npm run type-check passes
npm run lint passes
npm run test passes
npm run test:e2e passes
userStories.notes:
Files to modify:
- e2e/side-panel-plugin-tool-groups.e2e.ts (new file)
- plugins/e2e-test/src/index.ts — may need to add group assignments to e2e-test plugin tools if they don't already have them

The e2e-test plugin needs to have at least 2 different groups assigned to its tools for this test to be meaningful. Check if the e2e-test plugin tools already have 'group' field set in their defineTool calls. If not, add groups to at least 4-6 tools across 2 groups.

The test should specifically verify that group headers are NOT interactive — they should not contain any <button>, <select>, <input>, or Switch elements. This is important because the old design had a Switch in the group header, and the new design removed it. The test prevents the Switch from being accidentally re-added.
